text.title: Podcast Guest Appearance Strategy
text.summary: Finding relevant podcasts, pitching effectively, and converting appearances into leads and customers.

rule.statement: Default to guest appearances before hosting a show; guest strategy delivers faster ROI with zero equipment investment.
rule.rationale: Guest appearances reach existing audiences immediately. A single niche podcast appearance can produce 150+ trial signups and 30 paying customers. Hosting requires 12-24 months to deliver equivalent authority.

list.item: Listen Notes API: search by topic, filter by episode count and listener score
Podmatch / MatchMaker.fm: guest-host matching platforms (manual workflow)
Competitor research: find where similar founders or experts appear
Direct cold outreach to smaller niche shows (50K-500K listeners range)

rule.statement: Target podcasts with 50K-500K listeners in your niche; do not pitch 1M+ listener shows as a first step.
rule.rationale: Large shows require established credentials and relationships. Mid-tier niche shows have higher booking rates and more targeted audiences for B2B products.

section.title: Pitch Rules

rule.statement: Reference one specific recent episode by name in every pitch; a pitch without a named episode signals templating and is ignored.
rule.rationale: Personalization is the primary differentiator; hosts receive dozens of generic pitches weekly.

rule.statement: Lead with audience value, not the guest's credentials; propose 3 specific bullet points the episode will cover.
rule.rationale: Hosts prioritize audience benefit over guest status; credential-first pitches convert at one-third the rate of audience-value pitches.

section.reference: Guest pitch email with personalization slots and 200-word constraint

antipattern.description: Generic pitch with "I'm a huge fan of your show" opener and no episode reference.
antipattern.reason: Signals mass templating; hosts delete immediately. 200-word personalized pitch with specific episode reference outperforms by 3-5x.

antipattern.description: Preparing only one topic angle for the pitch.
antipattern.reason: Hosts often prefer a different angle; offering 3 distinct angles increases booking rate significantly.

antipattern.description: No CTA during the appearance (no mention of where to find more).
antipattern.reason: Listeners who are not directed to a specific action take none; every appearance needs a clear verbal CTA.

example.code: Approach: Pitched 30 niche podcasts, appeared on 15, shared unique framework each time Results: 2,500 website visits, 150 trial signups, 30 paying customers ($6K MRR), several partnership leads
